description: This figure presents Electron Backscatter Diffraction (EBSD) inverse pole figures and corresponding phase maps of 15Al Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steel under different aging treatments. Subfigures a1 (low magnification, 100 μm scale) and a2 (high magnification, 5 μm scale) show the sample aged at 525 °C for 3 h, where massive β-Mn phase precipitates with a small amount of residual BCC α matrix. Subfigures b1 (200 μm scale) and b2 (5 μm scale) correspond to the sample aged at 550 °C for 6 h, where α matrix almost fully transforms to β-Mn, accompanied by fine new BCC precipitates inside β-Mn. Subfigures c1 (200 μm scale) and c2 (5 μm scale) show the sample aged at 650 °C for 3 h, where no residual α is detected and new FCC phase precipitates distribute in β-Mn matrix. The results demonstrate that aging parameters (temperature, duration) regulate phase evolution of the alloy, and β-Mn precipitation acts as the main origin of the strong aging hardening effect in this steel system.
